data.title: HSM market to reach $3.51Bn by 2031, growing at 10% CAGR due to increasing adoption of hardware security models and data protection mandates.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, on January 30, 2026, a report from Mordor Intelligence revealed that the market for hardware security modules (HSM) is rapidly growing. The surge in demand is driven by enterprises focusing on enhancing cryptographic security, safeguarding data, and ensuring adherence to regulatory standards within their digital environments. With the HSM market witnessing significant expansion, this trend underscores the increasing importance placed on cybersecurity measures.

data.title: Wall Street analysts adjust Charter Communications' Q4 expectations. Company expected to report earnings of $9.88 per share, down from $10.1 in the year-ago period. Revenue estimate: $13.73 billion. Charter issued $3.0 billion in unsecured notes. Stock rose 4.8% to $191.52.
data.description: On Friday, January 30, Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings. Analysts predict earnings of $9.88 per share, a decrease from $10.1 per share in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for quarterly revenue is $13.73 billion, slightly lower than the previous year’s reported $13.93 billion. Recently, the company finalized $3.0 billion in senior unsecured notes. Closing at $191.52 on Thursday, Charter Communications' stock rose by 4.8%. For more detailed information and analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.

data.title: Bitcoin, with RSI at 30.63, eyes $95,000 recovery by Feb. Technical signals point to $86,816 resistance.
data.description: The Relative Strength Index (RSI) for Bitcoin stands at 30.63, indicating that the cryptocurrency is currently oversold. Technical analysis forecasts a possible comeback, with projections targeting a resistance level of $86,816 and an ultimate goal of $95,000 by the end of February 2026.

data.title: El Salvador buys gold and Bitcoin dips, purchasing 9,298 ounces of gold worth $50 million and boosting total reserves to 67,403 ounces valued at $360 million. President Bukele supports the purchase.
data.description: El country of El Salvador has made purchases in both Bitcoin and gold, following its unique approach of increasing reserves in these assets simultaneously. The Central Reserve Bank of El Salvador confirmed the acquisition of 9,298 ounces of gold, amounting to $50 million. This move has raised the total gold reserves to 67,403 ounces, valued at $360 million. The country's Central Bank stated that this purchase strengthens its "long-term equity" and ensures a "prudent balance" in its foreign reserves composition. President Nayib Bukele expressed enthusiasm for the acquisition, announcing, "We just bought this other dip." This purchase is part of El Salvador's ongoing strategy to diversify its reserves.

data.title: $400M forfeiture finalized by DOJ in Helix Bitcoin dark web case. Seized assets transferred to US gov, closing case from 2014-2017.
data.description: The US government has been granted legal ownership of seized cryptocurrency and assets as per a recent court ruling. This decision comes after a case linked to Helix’s activities between 2014 and 2017 has concluded. This transfer signifies the end of legal proceedings regarding the mentioned cryptocurrency and assets.

data.title: American Express prepares for Q4 earnings release. Analysts predict $3.54 EPS, $18.88 billion revenue. Stock rose 0.4% to close at $358.50.
data.description: American Express Company will announce its Q4 earnings on Friday, Jan. 30. Analysts predict earnings of $3.54 per share, up from $3.04 last year. Revenue is expected to be around $18.88 billion, compared to $17.18 billion last year. American Express has surpassed revenue estimates for the last three quarters and five out of the past 10. The stock price rose by 0.4% to $358.50 on Thursday. For more details, including the latest analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.

data.title: Wedbush analyst Dan Ives bullish on Tesla (TSLA) and Nvidia (NVDA) as top firms in physical AI market, forecasting $800 bull case for Tesla with FSD tech leading the way.
data.description: Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives reaffirms his positive outlook on the "fourth industrial revolution," emphasizing Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) and Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) as key players in the growing physical AI market. Ives highlights the shift towards machines interacting with the physical world and identifies Tesla and Nvidia as leaders in this transition. He praises Tesla's software advancement, particularly the Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology, predicting a potential surge in adoption from 12% to 50%, with a bullish case valuation of $800. This shift signifies a move from focusing on vehicle delivery counts to high-margin software adoption for Tesla. Ives indicates that these factors position Tesla and Nvidia as dominant players in the physical AI sector.

data.title: Argus introduces SAF and HVO assessments in southeast Asia, enhancing transparency in emerging markets.
data.description: A leading global energy and commodity price reporting agency, Argus, announced the introduction of the world's inaugural assessed prices for sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and hydrotreated vegetable biofuels in Southeast Asia. This development, unveiled in Singapore on Jan. 30, 2026, aims to enhance transparency in emerging markets. The initiative is set to provide reliable pricing information for these environmentally friendly fuel sources, catering to the growing demand for sustainable alternatives. This move marks a significant step in promoting eco-friendly practices in the region and beyond.
The Israeli Air Force struck a group of eight terror operatives who emerged from a tunnel in eastern Rafah in the southern Gaza Strip overnight, the military says.

data.title: Nasdaq drops 150 points, Investor sentiment down, Fear and Greed index in 'Greed' zone, Microsoft falls 10%, IBM rises 10%.
data.description: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index signaled a drop in market sentiment, remaining in the "Greed" territory on Thursday. In the U.S., stocks had a mixed performance, with the Nasdaq Composite experiencing a decline of over 150 points. The Federal Reserve opted to keep interest rates steady during its January FOMC meeting. Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ: MSFT) saw a nearly 10% decrease, marking its worst day since March 2020 due to a slowdown in cloud growth as reported for the recent quarter. ServiceNow Inc. (NYSE: NOW) also had a 10% decline despite positive earnings and revenue in the fourth quarter. Contrastingly, Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ: META) saw a surge of over 10% after issuing a positive sales outlook for the first quarter. International Business Machines Corp. (NYSE: IBM) was also part of the market activity. For the full story, please refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: iQIYI filed 880 invention patents in 2025, over half from AI innovation, totaling 993 new patents. #innovationleader
data.description: In Beijing on January 30, 2026, iQIYI, a top online entertainment platform in China, announced the publication of its 2025 annual patent report, highlighting a year of remarkable innovation. The company submitted a total of 993 new patents in the previous year, comprising 880 invention patents and 12 high-value ones.
